text:
Nintendo Switch 2 eShop charts – May 10, 2026 – Pokemon Pokopia holds on strong, Mixtape debuts

The latest Nintendo Switch 2 eShop charts for the week of May 10, 2026 are now available. We’ve been saying this for awhile now, but yes, it’s still Pokemon Pokopia at #1. For new games, Mixtape manages to crack the top three. Below are the full Nintendo Switch 2 eShop charts for the week of May 10, 2026: All Games...

text:
Mario Kart World dev discusses how the map was designed

What was it like designing the map for Mario Kart World? One Nintendo developer who worked on the game has shared some insight. author: Nintendo Everything
text:
[Let’s Talk] Are you sold on Star Fox for Nintendo Switch 2?

Things were a bit quiet on the Nintendo front last week and this week due to holidays in Japan, but that changed very quickly towards the end of Wednesday. Out of nowhere, the company announced and immediately broadcasted a Star Fox Direct, which revealed a remake of Star Fox 64 for Nintendo Switch 2. The title is coming very soon...

The post [Let’s Talk] Are you sold on Star Fox for Nintendo Switch 2? appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Wax Heads launch trailer – cozy-punk indie sim hits Nintendo Switch

To celebrate the launch of Wax Heads, Curve Games and Patattie Games have uploaded a launch trailer. The love letter to music culture, which comes in the form of a cozy-punk indie sim, arrived on Nintendo Switch this week. All of the important details can be found in the following overview: In a small northern town lies Repeater Records, a...

text:
Star Fox for Nintendo Switch 2 reveals amiibo support

The new Star Fox game for Switch 2 will feature amiibo support, according to Nintendo. We got plenty of the details about the title this week, but news about amiibo functionality went under the radar. To be fair, it’s a bit hidden – there’s only a brief mention of it over on the official Japanese website. When you scan a...

text:
List of all Nintendo Switch demos

Thousands of games are currently available on Nintendo Switch, and there are a ton of demos out there for many of them. Not just Nintendo, but also third-parties are offering demos. We’ve now put together a comprehensive list of what’s out there. Since our reader base is English-speaking, that’s what we’re focusing on here. In other words, we’re highlighting Switch...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Next Dragon Quest and more to be announced this month

Big Dragon Quest news is on the way including the announcement of the series’ next game. That’s according to Yuji Horii, the series’ creator. During a KosoKoso podcast, which is now private, Horii said that a live stream will take place on May 27, 2026. It’s here where Square Enix will “be able to announce the next title/work.” Horii also...

The post Next Dragon Quest and more to be announced this month appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Menace from the Deep: Complete Edition announced for Nintendo Switch

Feardemic is partnering with Flat Lab to bring Menace from the Deep: Complete Edition to Nintendo Switch, it’s been announced. The game comes out on May 14, 2026. Menace from the Deep is a roguelike deckbuilder that takes place in a myth-soaked 1920s USA. For the Complete Edition on Nintendo’s console, the The Rift of Sanity is packed in. More...

The post Menace from the Deep: Complete Edition announced for Nintendo Switch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Cheeky adventure game Devil Connection coming to Nintendo Switch

Devil Connection will be appearing on Nintendo Switch, Playism and ChaoGames revealed today. The game launches later in 2026. Devil Connection is a devilishly cheeky adventure title. After previously appearing in PC only in Japanese, it will come to Nintendo Switch in English for the first time (plus Traditional and Simplified Chinese as well). There’ll also be optimized controller support....

The post Cheeky adventure game Devil Connection coming to Nintendo Switch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.
